REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT (CCP 872.720) IS DENIED. THE REAL PROPERTY PARTITION ACT APPLIES TO ALL ACTIONS FOR PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY FILED ON OR AFTER JANUARY 1, 2023, WHERE THE REAL PROPERTY IS HELD IN TENANCY IN COMMON AND THERE IS NO AGREEMENT IN A RECORD BINDING ALL THE COTENANTS THAT GOVERNS THE PARTITION OF THE PROPERTY. (CCP SECTION 874.311, SUBDS. (B)-(C).) PLAINTIFF APPLIES FOR AN INTERLOCUTORY JUDGEMENT UNDER CCP SECTION 872.720. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY AUTHORITY THAT THE COURT MAY ISSUE AN INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT AT THIS STAGE AND STILL COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF THE REAL PROPERTY PARTITION ACT. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT EVEN ADDRESS THE REAL PROPERTY PARTITION ACT ON REPLY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC RULE 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment (Ccp 872.720)
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT (CCP 872.720) is DENIED. The Real Property Partition Act applies to all actions for partition of real property filed on or after January 1, 2023, where the real property is held in tenancy in common and there is no agreement in a record binding all the cotenants that governs the partition of the property. (CCP section 874.311, subds. (b)-(c).) Plaintiff applies for an Interlocutory Judgement under CCP section 872.720. Plaintiff has not provided any authority that the Court may issue an interlocutory judgment at this stage and still comply with the provisions of the Real Property Partition Act. The Court notes that Plaintiff did not even address the Real Property Partition Act on reply.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Set For Trial On Oct-06-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
